description: Represents an optional value. Every Option is either Some (contains a value) or None. Python equivalent: value or None, but type-safe.

methods.name: unwrap
methods.description: Returns the contained value. Panics if None.

methods.name: unwrap_or
methods.description: Returns the contained value or a default.

methods.name: map
methods.signature: fn map<U>(self, f: FnOnce(T) -> U) -> Option<U>
methods.description: Maps an Option<T> to Option<U> by applying a function.
methods.example:
let x = Some(5);
let y = x.map(|v| v * 2); // Some(10)

methods.name: is_some
methods.description: Returns true if the option is a Some value.

methods.name: is_none
methods.description: Returns true if the option is None.

methods.name: and_then
methods.signature: fn and_then<U>(self, f: FnOnce(T) -> Option<U>) -> Option<U>
methods.description: Returns None if None, otherwise calls f and returns the result. Like flatMap.
methods.example:
let x = Some(2);
let y = x.and_then(|v| if v > 0 { Some(v) } else { None });
